title: Warm up
content: The slide introduces three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—through images depicting drivers viewing each city from inside a car. Each image reflects a different aspect of urban life and technology use in that city.
key_claims: Beijing is shown with modern skyscrapers, traffic, and a dash cam, suggesting technology and urban development.
Chongqing is depicted with traditional architecture and a driver reading a book, suggesting tourism and cultural heritage.
Hangzhou is shown with a driver using a smartphone with AI/robot graphics, suggesting technology integration and smart innovation.

title: An Evolving Nation: A Ride Through China
content: This is a title slide introducing the presentation's theme of exploring China's development through a metaphorical taxi ride. It features images of a taxi sign and iconic Chinese landmarks representing different cities.
key_claims: The presentation is themed 'A Ride Through China'.
Images include a taxi sign, the Forbidden City (Beijing), a riverside town (Chongqing), and a pagoda (Hangzhou).

title: Session 1: Match the City Chain! - Investigate and Research
content: This slide outlines the structure of Session 1, which involves investigating and researching city development. It presents a three-step process flow using an illustrated cityscape graphic.
key_claims: The session follows a three-step process: Local Features, Development Strategies, and Outcomes & Positive Impact.
The process flows sequentially from identifying local features to strategies to outcomes.

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: This is an interactive matching exercise slide connecting three Chinese cities (Beijing, Chongqing, Hangzhou) to their respective development strategies. Lines show the correct matches between cities and strategies, accompanied by representative images.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with Technology & tradition integration (融合).
Chongqing is matched with Tourism & Economic growth.
Hangzhou is matched with Sustainable urban development.

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide summarizes the development strategies of the three cities as seen through taxi driver perspectives, reinforcing the theme that each city tailors its strategy to local conditions. It concludes with a key takeaway about China's adaptive development approach.
key_claims: Beijing's driver represents sustainable urban development.
Chongqing's driver represents tourism & economic growth.
Hangzhou's driver represents technology & tradition integration.
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote city development (因地制宜).
